qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Open redirect vulnerability in questionnaire templates controller
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes stored XSS in the user name field (decidim-core, CWE-79). This finding is an open redirect in the questionnaire templates controller (decidim-templates). Different vulnerability class, different component, different root cause, and different attack vector.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in participatory process import allows internal network access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes stored XSS in the user name field (decidim-core, CWE-79). This finding is SSRF in the participatory process import functionality (decidim-participatory_processes). Different vulnerability class (SSRF vs XSS), different component, different root cause (unvalidated URL fetching vs insufficient output escaping), different attack surface.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via ApplicationUploader remote_url assignment
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes stored XSS in the user name field (decidim-core, CWE-79). This finding is SSRF via ApplicationUploader.remote_url= (decidim-core uploader but different vulnerability class). Different root cause (unvalidated URL fetching vs insufficient output escaping in user name rendering), different attack surface, different exploitation path. No shared vulnerable code path with the advisory XSS.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-fc46-r95f-hq7g) describes a stored XSS vulnerability in the user name field of Decidim (decidim-core, CWE-79) where a low-privileged attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes when other users view comment pages. None of the three qualifying findings identify this root cause. vuln_open_redirect describes an open redirect in the questionnaire templates controller (decidim-templates). vuln_ssrf_import and vuln_ssrf_uploader describe SSRF vulnerabilities in remote URL fetching paths (decidim-participatory_processes and decidim-core ApplicationUploader). All three findings are different vulnerability classes (open redirect, SSRF) with different root causes (unvalidated redirect target, unvalidated URL fetching) than the advisory-described XSS (insufficient output escaping of user name in comment rendering).
evidence: Advisory: stored XSS in user name field, CWE-79, decidim-core
vuln_open_redirect: open redirect in questionnaire templates controller (decidim-templates), different vuln class and component
vuln_ssrf_import: SSRF in participatory process import (decidim-participatory_processes), different vuln class and component
vuln_ssrf_uploader: SSRF via ApplicationUploader remote_url=, same package (decidim-core) but different vuln class and root cause
No finding describes insufficient escaping/sanitization of user name in comment page rendering
